title: Shader Forge Reference Guide
intro: Searchable operator and assistant wiki for the current Shader Forge shell, session backend, native runtime, tooling, content data, and deterministic verification workflow.

categories.title: Getting Started
categories.description: Find the main surfaces quickly, understand where the live reference lives, and know which current docs should anchor day-to-day work.

pages.title: Reference Sources
pages.summary: Use the repo-native guide files first so terminal assistants, shell users, and later native assistants all resolve the same current guidance.

sections.title: Primary Sources
items: Use `docs/reference/ENGINE-REFERENCE-GUIDE.md` as the plain-text guide for terminal assistants and repo search.
Use `docs/reference/ENGINE-REFERENCE-GUIDE.json` as the structured guide source for the shell and future native assistants.
Use `plans/ENGINE-IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md` to check current phase order, progress, and dependency gates.
Use `docs/specs/ENGINE-SYSTEMS-INDEX.md` to jump to the subsystem specs that define the current architecture.
Use `AGENTS.md` for repo workflow rules, documentation obligations, and required update discipline.

sections.title: Assistant Lookup Workflow
items: Start with the markdown guide when you need a quick terminal-readable overview of current behavior.
Use the JSON guide when an assistant needs structured categories, page ids, references, or search terms.
Use the implementation plan and subsystem specs to widen from current behavior into target architecture.

sections.title: Update Discipline
items: When user-facing behavior, shell workflow, runtime control, or assistant-facing engine behavior changes, update the guide in the same pass.
Treat the guide as operator and assistant working documentation, not marketing copy or a post-hoc changelog.
Keep the guide concrete enough that a coding assistant in a terminal can search it and act on it without needing the whole codebase first.

pages.title: Shell Workspace Overview
pages.summary: The React shell is the current operator workspace for sessions, file browsing, source control, runtime control, and the preserved code bridge.

sections.title: Primary Shell Surfaces
items: The left rail currently exposes `Sessions`, `Explorer`, and `Source Control`.
The center dock currently exposes `Code`, `Game`, `Scene`, `Preview`, and `Guide`.
The right panel currently exposes `Details`, `Build`, and `Run`.
The bottom dock currently exposes `Terminal`, `Logs`, and `Output`.

sections.title: Current Workflow Shape
items: Use `Code` when you need the preserved Monaco bridge and repo workspace context.
Use `Game` and `Preview` when you are driving the external native runtime window from the shell.
Use `Scene` to track the text-backed authoring direction for `.scene.toml` and `.prefab.toml` assets.
Use `Guide` for the in-app wiki and repo source references.
sections.note: The shell is still the primary workspace even though the native SDL3/Vulkan runtime renders in an external window.

categories.title: Session Backend And CLI
categories.description: The shell is backed by `engine_sessiond` and the `engine` CLI, so sessions, files, git, build, and runtime operations all share the same local control plane.

pages.title: Sessions, Files, And Source Control
pages.summary: Session creation, safe file reads, host-root picking, and git status are already implemented and consumed by the shell.

sections.title: Implemented Backend Surfaces
items: `engine_sessiond` currently provides session create/list/get/update/delete.
Safe file list and file read APIs are already used by the `Explorer` rail.
Host filesystem directory listing is already used by the workspace-root picker.
Git status and repository initialization are already used by the `Source Control` rail.
PTY terminal lifecycle is already wired into the bottom-dock terminal surfaces.

sections.title: Shell Usage
items: Create or edit sessions from the `Sessions` rail.
Browse files and preview text content from the `Explorer` rail.
Inspect branch state and working tree changes from `Source Control`.

pages.title: Engine CLI Surfaces
pages.summary: The current CLI is focused on backend and runtime bring-up rather than broad content tooling.

sections.title: Implemented Commands
items: `engine sessiond start` starts the local backend service.
`engine session create` and `engine session list` expose session bring-up from the terminal.
`engine file list` and `engine file read` expose safe file inspection.
`engine build runtime` configures and builds the native runtime with CMake.
`engine run <scene>` builds and launches the native runtime and now forwards content, data, and tooling roots.

sections.title: Current Non-Goals
items: `engine import`, `engine bake`, `engine package`, and `engine export` are still later phases.
The CLI is not yet the main content pipeline surface because the cook/import lane is still being built.

categories.title: Runtime And Authoring
categories.description: The native runtime is past pure scaffolding, and the content data lanes now affect runtime startup behavior for real.

pages.title: Runtime Bring-Up And Viewer Bridge
pages.summary: The runtime opens a real SDL3/Vulkan window when local dependencies exist, while the shell owns build/run orchestration and diagnostics.

sections.title: Native Runtime
items: The native runtime has Vulkan instance, surface, device, swapchain, render pass, framebuffer, submit, and present bring-up.
Resize-aware swapchain recreation is already implemented.
Runtime build, play, stop, restart, pause, and resume are already controlled from the shell.
The shell tracks runtime state, build state, bridge activity, and recent log tails in `Game` and `Preview`.

sections.title: Current Viewer Boundary
items: The native runtime still renders in an external window.
The browser shell remains the primary workspace.
Embedded viewer transport and screenshot capture are still deferred until the native/runtime side is worth the added complexity.

pages.title: Scene, Prefab, And Data Foundation
pages.summary: Text-backed content roots now exist and runtime startup resolves scene/bootstrap metadata from them instead of treating scene names as free text only.

sections.title: Current Content Roots
items: `content/scenes/*.scene.toml` is the initial authored scene lane.
`content/prefabs/*.prefab.toml` is the initial authored prefab lane.
`content/data/*.data.toml` is the initial authored engine/bootstrap data lane.
`content/effects/*.effect.toml` is the initial authored effect-descriptor lane.

sections.title: Runtime Data Foundation
items: `data/foundation/engine-data-layout.toml` defines the current `TOML -> FlatBuffers -> SQLite` split.
The runtime validates the content roots through `DataFoundation` before startup continues.
Scene-to-prefab relationships are validated across the catalog.
`runtime_bootstrap.data.toml` can now provide a default scene and tooling overlay preference.
The runtime window title and startup logs now include active scene and primary prefab context from the authored assets.
sections.note: This is still a foundation slice. There is not yet a real FlatBuffers cook step, SQLite asset index, or Effekseer runtime integration.

categories.title: Input, Tooling, And Testing
categories.description: The engine has real named input plumbing, tooling-layout groundwork, and deterministic harness coverage that now needs to stay current as features widen.

pages.title: Input And Native Tooling Foundations
pages.summary: Gameplay, UI, and tooling controls are routed through named engine actions, while the native tooling layer has a panel registry and layout persistence groundwork.

sections.title: Input System
items: `input/actions.toml` plus `input/contexts/*.input.toml` define the current action/context maps.
Keyboard, mouse, and gamepad input are routed through engine-owned named actions and axes.
The runtime currently consumes actions such as `runtime_exit`, `move_x`, `move_y`, `look_x`, `look_y`, `ui_accept`, and `ui_back`.

sections.title: Tooling UI
items: The native tooling substrate currently has a named panel registry.
Tooling layouts are loaded from text and session layouts can be saved back to disk.
The current panel set covers runtime stats, input debug, log view, and debug state.
Tooling overlay and panel toggles are already bound through the engine-owned input actions.
sections.note: Dear ImGui docking and real in-process native panel rendering are still ahead. The current slice is substrate, not the final tooling frontend.

pages.title: Deterministic Harnesses And Clean Start
pages.summary: The repo uses deterministic harnesses as the default regression lane, and the clean-start scripts run the current baseline before starting the active workspace.

sections.title: Current Harnesses
items: `npm test` runs the preserved shell smoke harness.
`npm run test:sessiond` validates the local backend session and file flows.
`npm run test:viewer-bridge` validates build/runtime bridge events.
`npm run test:runtime-scaffold`, `test:input-scaffold`, `test:tooling-ui-scaffold`, and `test:data-foundation-scaffold` validate the native bring-up slices.

sections.title: Clean Start
items: `./scripts/start-dev-clean.sh` is the Unix/WSL clean-start path.
`powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\scripts\start-dev-clean.ps1` is the Windows clean-start path.
Both scripts remove generated outputs, rerun the current deterministic baseline, start `engine_sessiond`, and then launch the active shell workflow.

pages.title: Current Boundaries And Next Widening Passes
pages.summary: The project is moving through dependency-first slices; this guide should say clearly what exists today and what still needs widening.

sections.title: What Exists Now
items: A React/Vite shell workspace with backend-owned sessions, file preview, source control, terminal tabs, and runtime control.
A real native SDL3/Vulkan runtime slice with input, tooling, and data-foundation hooks.
Text-backed scene, prefab, data, and effect roots represented in the repo.
A searchable in-app guide and repo-native assistant guide.

sections.title: What Still Needs Widening
items: The shell still needs deeper UX and more app-native surfaces beyond the preserved code bridge.
The runtime still needs richer rendering, real scene loading, and broader native verification.
The content pipeline still needs real cook/import/export lanes, not only foundation decisions.
Tooling UI still needs the full Dear ImGui frontend and deeper authoring/profiling panels.
